name: Teishoku Restaurant
tagline: Japanese set meals in a Kalihi shopping center — miso butterfish, miso soup, rice, pickles, all for one price. Tourists will never find this.
mustOrder: miso butterfish teishoku — the signature set meal
chicken katsu set — crispy and satisfying
any daily special — the rotating items are where the kitchen shines
parking: Kamehameha Shopping Center lot on N School St
talkStory: Teishoku means 'set meal' in Japanese — a complete meal with miso soup, salad, pickled vegetables, rice, and your choice of protein, all on one tray. This is how locals eat lunch in Kalihi. N School Street is deep-neighborhood territory — no tourist has ever wandered here. Featured in Honolulu Magazine as a hidden gem. Fast service, very reasonable prices. Lunch and dinner, closed Mondays.
